mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month growth in industrial production signals broad economic expansion and improving corporate operating conditions, leading to positive short-term equity returns as earnings expectations are revised upwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sustained increase in the unemployment rate indicates a rapid deterioration in the labor market, a leading signal of recession that negatively impacts consumer demand and corporate profitability, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the policy rate remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This increases the probability of a future, more aggressive tightening cycle, creating policy uncertainty and compressing equity multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:Taylor Rule framework implies policy is too accommodative.
failure_condition: The interaction term between inflation surprise and low policy rates fails to show a statistically significant negative coefficient in a regression on 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong growth in retail sales has an amplified positive effect on equity returns during a post-recession recovery, as it confirms pent-up consumer demand is being unleashed, leading to faster-than-expected earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes ~70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: The signal's positive performance is not statistically different during post-shock recovery periods (defined by NBER) compared to other regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust and expanding economy. This directly translates to higher aggregate income and demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive equity market sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a key series watched by the Federal Reserve's FOMC.
failure_condition: A strategy going long SPX after positive PAYEMS surprises underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts is a leading indicator of economic slowdown, as the housing sector is highly sensitive to interest rates and consumer confidence. This weakness precedes broader economic trouble, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Residential investment is a volatile but leading component of GDP.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns, invalidating the signal.

mechanism_narrative: An inverted yield curve, proxied by the Fed Funds rate being higher than long-term growth expectations (related to real GDP), signals tight monetary policy that restricts economic activity, leading to future negative returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is higher than the 4-quarter moving average of YoY GDPC1 growth
evidence_refs: doc:Yield curve inversion as a recession predictor
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict a market downturn (i.e., S&P 500 does not fall >10%) within 18 months of a trigger in at least two consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflationary environment, characterized by falling industrial production (negative supply shock) and rising inflation, is exceptionally damaging for equities. It simultaneously pressures corporate margins and forces central banks to tighten policy into weakness.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY change < 0% and CPIAUCSL YoY change > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation was a prolonged bear market for equities.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) when the trigger condition is met.
